title: Ghost-Train Track
central_metaphor: A ghost-train track, representing the invisible, looping path of memory that leads back to a childhood world that has physically disappeared.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The screen door’s got a familiar whine
On a Tuesday dusk in ‘99
I’m staring at a rusted bicycle chain
That’s left a permanent stain on the lane
[Pre-Chorus]
The crickets start their evening song
And I know I don’t belong here long
[Chorus]
Just a ghost-train track
Running out and back
To a kingdom made of plywood and pine
On a borrowed piece of time
[Verse 2]
Now the developer’s sign says “Paradise Views”
Where we built our forts and scuffed our shoes
They’ll pour the concrete where the blackberries grew
And paint a line where home plate used to be true
[Pre-Chorus]
The streetlights buzz a different key
And there’s no shade left for me
[Chorus]
Just a ghost-train track
Running out and back
To a kingdom made of plywood and pine
On a borrowed piece of time
[Bridge]
I drove back to find a landmark, a stone
But the creek’s been rerouted, the tall grass is mown
The only thing left from the world that I knew
Is the echo of my name, if the wind blows through
[Final Chorus]
Just a ghost-train track
Running out and back
To a kingdom made of plywood and pine
On a **forever** piece of time
